title: Pound Cake Gets Its Pound of Fame
story: Every March 4, bakers and dessert lovers celebrate National Pound Cake Day by whipping up this classic treat! Traditional pound cake got its name because recipes once called for a whole pound each of butter, sugar, eggs, and flour. Today, people bake lighter versions and get creative with flavors like lemon, vanilla, or chocolate chip. Many families share their favorite pound cake recipes on social media or host baking parties to mark this sweet holiday.
activity_question: Why was pound cake originally called "pound cake"?
activity_choices: Because it weighed exactly one pound when finished
Because recipes used a pound each of four main ingredients
Because it cost one pound of money to make
Because you had to pound the batter for an hour
activity_answer: Because recipes used a pound each of four main ingredients
bonus_fact: Some pound cakes today use cream cheese or sour cream for extra moisture!

title: Hugs for Heroes Day
story: Every March 4, Americans celebrate National Hug a GI Day by showing appreciation for military service members. People give hugs, handshakes, and high-fives to soldiers, veterans, and anyone who has served in the armed forces. Families visit military bases, communities host appreciation events, and kids make thank-you cards. Some people wear patriotic colors or post messages on social media using special hashtags. It's a simple but powerful way to say "thank you" to the brave men and women who protect our country.
activity_question: What do people do on National Hug a GI Day to show appreciation?
activity_choices: Give hugs and make thank-you cards
activity_answer: Give hugs and make thank-you cards
follow_up_question: What other ways could you show appreciation for military service members?

title: What's in a Name? Everything!
story: Every March 4, people dive into the fascinating stories behind their names on Learn What Your Name Means Day! Kids and adults search online databases, ask family members, and explore name origin books to discover whether their name means "brave warrior," "bright star," or something totally unexpected. Some people find out their names come from ancient languages, while others learn they were named after famous people or places. Families share stories about why parents chose certain names, and social media fills with fun posts about name meanings and family traditions.
activity_question: What do people use to research their name meanings on this holiday?
follow_up_question: What do you think your name means, and who chose it for you?
